message:
fix(widgets): extend NoStyling/Skeleton degradation to decision_card, drift_visualization + more block/panel fixups

Eighth wave of the degradation cleanup.  Two more widgets
(decision_card, drift_visualization) baked their signal/badge
styles unconditionally, and block/panel got a few trailing
fix-ups on edge cases the earlier "block and panel" commit
missed (inner background fill + title style application).

crates/ftui-widgets/src/decision_card.rs

- render_signal_row(x, y, max_x, frame):
    - Snapshot deg = frame.buffer.degradation at entry.
    - Compute the effective badge_style as
        if deg.apply_styling() { signal_style } else { Style::default() }
      and the effective title_style similarly, before the
      draw_text_span calls.
    - The " OK " / " WARN " / " ALERT " badge is still rendered
      in every case — only the color and background are dropped
      under NoStyling, so the semantic "which signal is this"
      information is preserved (fallback to bracketed ASCII).
- render_explanation: the DIM_FG style used for the explanation
  line is now conditional on apply_styling().  Without this,
  the explanation would render in dim color under NoStyling
  (the lowest-priority text would still be visually deprioritized,
  which defeats the NoStyling accessibility contract).
- Widget::render: early-return on !deg.render_content() so
  Skeleton produces a full no-op.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/drift_visualization.rs

- Same pattern applied to the drift visualization's bar cells,
  axis labels, legend entries, and the baseline / current
  markers.  Each builder now consults a precomputed local
  style variable derived from apply_styling().
- render() early-returns on Skeleton.
- The drift magnitude color gradient (green → amber → red) is
  preserved under Full but falls back to a single default
  style under NoStyling.  Users relying on screen readers
  still get the structural bar glyphs and numeric labels.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/block.rs
crates/ftui-widgets/src/panel.rs

- block.rs: inner background fill (the cells inside the border,
  between the top/bottom edge and the title) now consults the
  runtime style snapshot that the earlier "block and panel"
  commit computed but only applied to the border and title
  paths.  The inner fill was still using self.background_style
  directly.
- panel.rs: the title-container background fill gets the same
  treatment.  Under NoStyling the background cell is now a
  plain space instead of a colored padding cell.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
